top_headlines.headline: Trump Claims Iran Has Requested Ceasefire, Sets Strait of Hormuz Reopening as Condition
top_headlines.summary: President Trump stated via social media that Iran's president has asked the U.S. for a ceasefire in the ongoing conflict, saying the request would only be considered after the Strait of Hormuz is reopened to shipping. Iran's IRGC countered that the strait remains 'fully' under its control, and Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi said he has 'no faith' in talks. Trump is scheduled to address the nation on the Iran war, while also suggesting the U.S. may reconsider its NATO membership over what he described as the alliance's insufficient support for the war effort.

top_headlines.headline: NASA Artemis II Crew Launches on First Crewed Lunar Mission Since 1972
top_headlines.summary: NASA launched its Artemis II mission on Wednesday, sending four astronauts — commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, astronaut Christina Koch, and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen — on a nine-day journey around the moon. The mission marks the first time humans have traveled toward the moon in over 50 years, and if successful will be the first to carry a woman, a person of color, and a non-American near the lunar surface. The crew is expected to splash down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10 and will not land on the moon but will fly a free-return trajectory around it.

top_headlines.headline: Federal Judge Rules Trump's Executive Order to Defund NPR and PBS Unconstitutional
top_headlines.summary: A U.S. District Court judge found that President Trump's executive order calling for the defunding of NPR and PBS violated the First Amendment. The ruling represents a legal setback for the administration's effort to cut public media funding. The decision is expected to face further legal challenge.

tech_ai.headline: SpaceX Reported to Have Filed S-1 With SEC Ahead of Landmark 2026 IPO
tech_ai.summary: Confidential reports indicate SpaceX has filed its S-1 registration paperwork with the SEC, targeting a public market valuation between $1.5 trillion and $1.75 trillion, with a potential debut as early as June 2026. The company, which recently absorbed Elon Musk's xAI at a combined $1.25 trillion valuation, would represent the largest IPO in history if it proceeds at that price. Index providers including S&P Dow Jones, FTSE Russell, and Nasdaq are reportedly considering fast-track rules to add SpaceX to major indices almost immediately after listing.

tech_ai.headline: OpenAI Surpasses $25 Billion in Annualized Revenue, Eyes Late-2026 Public Listing
tech_ai.summary: OpenAI has surpassed $25 billion in annualized revenue and is reportedly taking early steps toward a public listing potentially as soon as late 2026, having closed a funding round at a $730 billion valuation backed by Amazon, SoftBank, Nvidia, and Microsoft. The company has been transitioning to a for-profit Public Benefit Corporation structure to satisfy both public market fiduciary requirements and regulatory safety concerns. Rival Anthropic is approaching $19 billion in annualized revenue and is also widely expected to pursue an IPO in a similar timeframe.

tech_ai.headline: Iran's IRGC Issues Threat List Targeting Apple, Google, Nvidia and Other Tech Giants
tech_ai.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps released a list of 18 potential targets for retaliation — including major U.S. technology companies such as Apple, Google, Meta, and Nvidia — threatening strikes if further assassinations of Iranian leaders occur. The threat marks an escalation in Iran's rhetorical posture toward Western corporate and technology interests amid the ongoing conflict. Markets responded with initial volatility before recovering on hopes of a potential ceasefire.

business_finance.headline: IEA Warns Oil Supply Crunch Will Worsen in April as Hormuz Closure Enters Second Month
business_finance.summary: The International Energy Agency warned Thursday that the oil supply crunch will worsen in April if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, with analysts estimating the world has already lost 4.5–5 million barrels per day of supply — approximately 5% of global output. Oil industry executives have cautioned that the strait needs to reopen by mid-April or disruptions will escalate sharply, with Brent crude settling near $99 per barrel. The IEA has described the Hormuz closure as the 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.'

business_finance.headline: IMF Warns Iran War Will Drive Higher Prices and Slower Global Growth
business_finance.summary: The IMF issued a warning that the ongoing Middle East conflict stemming from the Iran war will lead to higher inflation and slower global economic growth, as energy supply disruptions ripple through international markets. European economies are particularly exposed, with the ECB having already postponed planned interest rate cuts and raised its 2026 inflation forecast. Experts have slashed 2026 and 2027 growth forecasts for Germany, Europe's largest economy, while governments across the continent are introducing measures to cushion consumers from rising energy prices.

business_finance.headline: Markets Rally as Iran Ceasefire Signals Lift Stocks; U.S. Gas Prices Top $4 a Gallon
business_finance.summary: U.S. equity markets rose sharply Thursday — with the Dow Jones gaining over 290 points and the Nasdaq up more than 1% — as Trump's reported ceasefire signal from Iran fueled a relief rally in stocks and Treasury yields. However, U.S. average gasoline prices topped $4 per gallon for the first time since 2022, driven by the Hormuz oil supply disruption. Crude oil traded near $99 per barrel as traders weighed the prospects of a negotiated end to the conflict against continued physical supply shortfalls.

world_news.headline: Lebanon Conflict Widens as Hezbollah, Houthis Launch Fresh Attacks on Israel
world_news.summary: Israel said it killed a senior Hezbollah commander in a strike on Beirut, as Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen launched a barrage of ballistic missiles at southern Israel in a joint operation with Iran and Hezbollah. At least 16 people, including a child, were wounded after Iranian missile fragments hit Israel's Tel Aviv and Bnei Brak. Israeli forces have intensified bombing in Lebanon since early March following Hezbollah cross-border attacks that began after the U.S.-Israeli joint assault on Iran.

world_news.headline: King Charles III to Proceed with April State Visit to the United States
world_news.summary: King Charles III is going ahead with a planned state visit to the United States in April, despite calls from some quarters for the ceremonial event to be cancelled amid diplomatic tensions. The visit is expected to coincide with celebrations of the 250th anniversary of American independence. Buckingham Palace confirmed the visit is proceeding as scheduled.

world_news.headline: Rohingya Refugees in Bangladesh Face Sharp Food Aid Cuts as Crisis Deepens
world_news.summary: Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps in Cox's Bazar are facing significant reductions in food assistance starting this week, raising alarm throughout the increasingly desperate community. The cuts affect one of the world's largest refugee populations, numbering over one million people in the camps. Humanitarian organizations have warned the reductions could have severe consequences for an already vulnerable population.

music.headline: BTS Tops Billboard 200 and Hot 100 Simultaneously With Comeback Album 'ARIRANG'
music.summary: BTS scored their seventh No. 1 album on the Billboard 200 with 'ARIRANG,' earning 641,000 equivalent album units in its opening week — the biggest week for any group album in more than a decade. Lead single 'Swim' simultaneously debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100, making BTS only the second act ever to top both charts at the same time, a feat they first achieved in 2020 with 'BE.' All 13 eligible tracks from the 14-song album charted on the Hot 100, and BTS also became the first artist to claim the entire top 10 of the Billboard Global Excl. U.S. chart with songs from a single release. The group's world tour in support of the album launches April 9 in Goyang, South Korea.

music.headline: RAYE's Critically Acclaimed Second Album 'This Music May Contain Hope' Earns 88 Critic Score
music.summary: RAYE's second studio album 'This Music May Contain Hope' has been met with widespread critical acclaim, earning an 88 critic score and emerging as one of the highest-rated albums of 2026 so far. The British singer-songwriter, who independently released her acclaimed debut 'My 21st Century Blues' in 2023, is currently touring the album on her 'This Tour May Contain New Music' run. The album follows viral singles including 'Where Is My Husband!', which was named one of NME's best songs of 2025.

music.headline: Mitski Kicks Off 'Nothing's About to Happen to Me' World Tour in Los Angeles
music.summary: Mitski begins a three-night residency at Hollywood High in Los Angeles on April 2, opening her 2026 global tour in support of new album 'Nothing's About to Happen to Me.' The indie artist released the album's second single, 'I'll Change For You,' alongside the tour announcement, following the lead single 'Where's My Phone?'. The extensive tour will subsequently take Mitski to Istanbul, Paris, Amsterdam, London's Royal Albert Hall, Sydney Opera House, and multiple dates across Southeast Asia.
